data.narrative:
Portal Space Systems is a Series A space and aerospace company that has raised $62.85M to date. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comparable universe of 4 private peers at similar or adjacent stages (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network), with valuations ranging from $400M to $2.0B, alongside 5 public aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two methods produced quantifiable results. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $63M total raised proxy, yielding $126M. Peer-median valuation across the 4-company cohort produces $850M, with peers distributed between $400M and $2.0B. The public-comp anchoring method, while relevant given the sector median P/S of 42.9x across public names, cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $107M to $977M, with a median implied value of $488M. This wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around stage-appropriate comparables—peer valuations span more than 5x—and the nascent, capital-intensive nature of the space sector. Key uncertainties include Portal's specific technology differentiation relative to peers, near-term capital requirements and burn profile, and whether the Series B peer set (which dominates the comp universe) appropriately anchors a Series A valuation.
